content.problemStatement:
The binary number system uses $2$ digits - or $0$, $1$ bits. \
Whereas, the decimal number system uses $10$ digits.

Say, we have a number in binary number system - $1001110$. \
What is the value of this number?

$10011101 = 1 \cdot 2^7 + 0 \cdot 2^6 + 0 \cdot 2^5 + 1 \cdot 2^4 + 1 \cdot 2^3 + 1 \cdot 2^2 + 0 \cdot 2^1 + 1 \cdot 2^0$ \
$10011101 = 157$ in decimal number system.

Note that in all the number systems, a number with more digits is always greater than the one with less digits (after removing leading zeroes).

So, $111...1$ ($x$ digits) is always smaller than $100...0$ ($y$ digits) if $x < y$.

The leftmost bit is called the Most Significant Bit or MSB. \
The rightmost bit is called the Least Significant Bit or LSB. \
This is because of the value they carry in the number.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 1000$
- $2 \leq N \leq 10^5$
- $1 \leq A_i \leq 10^9$
- The sum of $N$ across all test cases does not exceed $10^5$
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
- The first line of each test case contains a single integer $N$, denoting the length of array $A$.
- The second line of each test case contains $N$ space-separated integers $A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N$ — denoting the array $A$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the maximum value of $A_1+A_N$ you can get after several right rotations.
